info.title: École Platform API
info.description:
EdTech SaaS platform API for K-12 schools in Morocco.

## Domains
- **IAM** — Authentication, authorization, invitations, recovery, 2FA
- **ERP** — Classes, enrollments, teacher assignments, attendance
- **LMS** — Courses, assignments, submissions, results, content, activities, assessments
- **Billing** — Invoices and payments
- **COM** — Notifications, consents, parent feed
- **AI** — Writing assistance, recommendations, KPIs

## Authentication
All protected endpoints require a `Bearer` access token in the `Authorization` header.
Tokens are obtained via `POST /auth/login`.

get.description:
Health check endpoint.

Returns service status and version. Used by Docker health checks
and monitoring systems (Pack F2).

post.description:
Authenticate user and create a session.

Returns access token in response body.
Sets refresh token as HttpOnly/Secure/SameSite=Lax cookie.
Sets CSRF token as non-HttpOnly cookie (for double-submit pattern).

Register a new account using an invitation code.

Creates user + membership + role-specific profile in one transaction.
Returns JWT tokens so the user is logged in immediately.
Rate limited: 5 registrations per 15 minutes per IP (auth category).

Refresh the access token using a valid refresh token from the HttpOnly cookie.

Requires X-CSRF-Token header matching the double-submit cookie value.
Implements token rotation: old refresh token invalidated, new one issued.

Revoke the user's session and clear authentication cookies.

Idempotent: calling logout on an already-revoked session returns 200.

get.description:
Return the authenticated user's profile with permissions and memberships.

This endpoint is the frontend's source of truth for RBAC UI gating.

200.description: User profile with permissions and memberships

get.description:
List the authenticated user's active sessions with device info.

Returns sessions with: session_id, source, user_agent, ip_address,
device_name, created_at, last_active.

200.description: List of active sessions with device info

delete.description:
Revoke a specific session by ID.

Users can revoke their own sessions. ADM can revoke any session in the school.
Invalidates the refresh token and clears the session from Redis.

post.description:
Change the authenticated user's password.

Requires current password verification. Enforces password policy on new password.
Revokes all other active sessions (keeps current session).

Generate a new TOTP secret and QR code provisioning URI.

Returns the secret and URI for the authenticator app.
2FA is not yet active — call /auth/2fa/verify-setup with a valid code to activate.

Verify first TOTP code to activate 2FA.

Returns 10 single-use backup codes (shown once, store securely).
Disable 2FA. Requires a valid TOTP code or backup code.

Verify TOTP code during login flow.

Called after login returns requires_2fa=true.
Accepts temp_token + TOTP code (or backup code) and returns full tokens.
Verify email address via OTP sent during invite consumption.

Create an invitation code for onboarding new users.

Returns the plaintext code (shown once, never stored).

Consume an invitation code to join a school with the specified role.

Idempotent: same code + same user = returns existing membership.

Soft-revoke an invitation code.

Idempotent: revoking an already-revoked code is a no-op.

Request account recovery. Always returns 200 to prevent email enumeration.

If user exists: creates recovery request and generates OTP (logged in dev).
If user doesn't exist: returns success anyway.

200.description: Recovery request ID (always 200, no email enumeration)

post.description:
Verify recovery OTP. Transitions status: pending -> verified.

Lockout after 5 failed attempts (30-minute lock).

Reset user's password. Requires status=verified.

Revokes all active sessions for the user (force re-login).
State machine: pending -> verified -> reset (no backward transitions).

get.description:
Get class details by ID.

Full security pipeline:
1. AuthN — verified by get_current_user (via requires_permission)
2. RBAC — requires PERM-ERP:class:read
3. ABAC — school boundary (404 for other school's classes)
4. ABAC — teacher assignment (TCH only sees assigned classes)

200.description: Class with capacity, level, academic year

post.description:
Enroll a student in a class.

Validates:
- Student exists and is in the same school
- Class exists and is in the same school
- Period is active
- INV-ERP-CLASS-ACTIVE: one active enrollment per student per period (409)

Idempotent: re-enrolling same student in same class returns existing enrollment.

Assign a teacher to a class for a period.

Validates:
1. Teacher exists and is in the same school
2. Class exists and is in the same school
3. Period exists and is in the same school
4. No duplicate assignment (idempotent if same tuple exists)

Create an attendance session with records for a class.

Validates:
1. Class exists and is in the same school
2. Teacher is assigned to the class (ABAC)
3. Period is active
4. No duplicate session for same class/date/slot (409)
5. Creates attendance records for each student

Submit an absence justification for a student.

Validates:
1. Attendance record exists and is in the same school
2. Student is absent/late (only those can be justified)
3. Parent-child ownership (ABAC)
4. No duplicate justification for same record (idempotent)

Review an absence justification (approve or reject).

Validates:
1. Justification exists and is in the same school
2. Justification is still pending
3. If rejecting, rejection_reason is required
4. Updates justification status + creates review record

Create a course for a class.

Validates:
1. Class exists and is in the same school
2. Teacher is assigned to the class (ABAC)
3. Creates course

get.description:
List courses with filtering, sorting, and full-text search.

TCH: only sees courses for assigned classes.
Filters: ?filter[status]=published&filter[title__like]=math
Sort: ?sort=-created_at,title
Search: ?search=mathematiques

parameters.name: sort

schema.description: Sort fields, comma-separated. Prefix with - for desc. E.g. -created_at,title
schema.title: Sort

parameters.description: Sort fields, comma-separated. Prefix with - for desc. E.g. -created_at,title

parameters.name: search

schema.description: Full-text search term. Searches across title, description, body fields.
schema.title: Search

parameters.description: Full-text search term. Searches across title, description, body fields.

post.description:
Create an assignment for a course.

Validates:
1. Course exists and is in the same school
2. Teacher owns the course
3. Creates assignment

get.description:
List assignments with filtering, sorting, and full-text search.

Filters: ?filter[title__like]=math&filter[total_points__gte]=10
Sort: ?sort=-due_at,title
Search: ?search=homework
Legacy param course_id still supported.

parameters.name: sort

schema.description: Sort fields, comma-separated. Prefix with - for desc. E.g. -created_at,title
schema.title: Sort

parameters.description: Sort fields, comma-separated. Prefix with - for desc. E.g. -created_at,title

parameters.name: search

schema.description: Full-text search term. Searches across title, description, body fields.
schema.title: Search

parameters.description: Full-text search term. Searches across title, description, body fields.

post.summary: Upload exercise PDF for a PRINTABLE_PDF assignment
post.description:
Upload or replace the exercise PDF for a PRINTABLE_PDF assignment.

Validates:
1. Assignment exists, teacher owns it
2. exercise_type must be PRINTABLE_PDF
3. File must be application/pdf

get.description:
Download the printable exercise PDF.

Access: teacher who owns the course, or student enrolled in the class.

post.description:
Create a submission for an assignment.

Validates:
1. Assignment exists
2. Course is in the same school
3. INV-LMS-SUBMISSION: one active submission per student per assignment (idempotent)

Grade a student submission.

Validates:
1. Submission exists
2. Assignment is in teacher's course (teacher owns the course)
3. Submission must be in 'submitted' status
4. Score <= assignment total_points
5. Creates grade, updates submission status to 'graded'

Upload a file to a submission.

Validates:
1. Submission exists and belongs to the student
2. Submission is in draft/submitted status
3. Max 5 files per submission
4. MIME type whitelist + file size limit
5. Computes SHA-256 checksum on write

get.description:
Download a submission file.

Access: student who owns the submission, teacher assigned to the course, or admin.

post.summary: Finalize and submit a draft submission
post.description:
Finalize a draft submission (transition draft → submitted).

For PRINTABLE_PDF assignments, validates that at least one file has been uploaded.

get.description:
Teacher inline preview: list submission files with metadata for rendering.

Returns file list with mime_type, file_type_hint, and download URL path
so the frontend can render inline previews for images/PDFs.

200.description: List of files with preview metadata

get.description:
List graded results for a student.

STD: sees own results (student_id ignored, uses auth.user_id).
PAR: must specify student_id, which must be a linked child.

200.description: Paginated list of grades and results

get.description:
List published content items with filtering, sorting, and full-text search.

Filters: ?filter[content_type]=video&filter[level_band]=college
Sort: ?sort=-created_at
Search: ?search=mathematiques
Legacy params content_type, level_band, language still supported.

parameters.name: sort

schema.description: Sort fields, comma-separated. Prefix with - for desc. E.g. -created_at,title
schema.title: Sort

parameters.description: Sort fields, comma-separated. Prefix with - for desc. E.g. -created_at,title

parameters.name: search

schema.description: Full-text search term. Searches across title, description, body fields.
schema.title: Search

parameters.description: Full-text search term. Searches across title, description, body fields.

get.description:
Get a content item by ID.

Only published items visible. School boundary or platform-wide.

post.description:
Update student progress on a content item.

Upsert: creates or updates progress record.
Unique per (student_id, content_item_id).

Upload a file asset to a content item.

Access: TCH (school-bound), ADM.
Validates MIME whitelist, file size limit, computes SHA-256 checksum.

get.description:
Download a content item asset.

Access: all authenticated users with content-asset:read (STD, PAR, TCH, ADM).
School boundary enforced for school-specific content.

delete.description:
Delete a content item asset.

Access: TCH (school-bound), ADM.
Removes from storage and database.

get.description:
List available activities with filtering, sorting, and full-text search.

Shows school-specific + platform-wide activities.
Filters: ?filter[type]=quiz&filter[difficulty]=easy
Sort: ?sort=-created_at
Search: ?search=multiplication

parameters.name: sort

schema.description: Sort fields, comma-separated. Prefix with - for desc. E.g. -created_at,title
schema.title: Sort

parameters.description: Sort fields, comma-separated. Prefix with - for desc. E.g. -created_at,title

parameters.name: search

schema.description: Full-text search term. Searches across title, description, body fields.
schema.title: Search

parameters.description: Full-text search term. Searches across title, description, body fields.

post.description:
Start a new activity session.

Validates:
1. Activity exists (school or platform-wide)
2. Creates session with incremented attempt_no

Complete an activity session with optional score.

Validates:
1. Session exists and belongs to the student
2. Session is in 'started' status
3. Updates status to 'completed' and sets score

Create a formal assessment for a class.

Validates:
1. Class exists and is in the same school
2. TCH: must be assigned to the class (ABAC)

get.description:
List assessments with filtering, sorting, and full-text search.

Filters: ?filter[status]=published&filter[title__like]=exam
Sort: ?sort=-due_at
Search: ?search=final
Legacy params class_id, status still supported.

parameters.name: sort

schema.description: Sort fields, comma-separated. Prefix with - for desc. E.g. -created_at,title
schema.title: Sort

parameters.description: Sort fields, comma-separated. Prefix with - for desc. E.g. -created_at,title

parameters.name: search

schema.description: Full-text search term. Searches across title, description, body fields.
schema.title: Search

parameters.description: Full-text search term. Searches across title, description, body fields.

post.description: Publish an assessment (change status from draft to published).

Submit a result for an assessment (student self-report or auto-graded).

Validates:
1. Assessment exists and is published
2. No duplicate result for same student+assessment (idempotent)

get.description:
List invoices with filtering, sorting, and full-text search.

PAR: sees own invoices only.
ADM: sees all invoices for the school.
Filters: ?filter[status]=paid&filter[total_amount__gte]=100
Sort: ?sort=-issued_date
Search: ?search=tuition
Legacy param status still supported.

parameters.name: sort

schema.description: Sort fields, comma-separated. Prefix with - for desc. E.g. -created_at,title
schema.title: Sort

parameters.description: Sort fields, comma-separated. Prefix with - for desc. E.g. -created_at,title

parameters.name: search

schema.description: Full-text search term. Searches across title, description, body fields.
schema.title: Search

parameters.description: Full-text search term. Searches across title, description, body fields.

get.description: Get invoice details by ID.

post.description:
Initiate a payment attempt for an invoice.

Validates:
1. Invoice exists, is in same school, belongs to the parent
2. Invoice is in pending status
3. Idempotency via idempotency_key (returns existing if same key)

get.description: Get payment attempt status.

post.description:
Handle a webhook event from a payment provider.

INV-BIL-WEBHOOK: duplicate provider_event_id = no-op (idempotent).

Flow:
1. Check for duplicate provider_event_id
2. Find matching payment attempt (optional)
3. Update payment attempt status if found
4. Update invoice status if payment is finalized
5. Store webhook event

get.description:
List notifications with filtering, sorting, and full-text search.

PAR: sees own notifications only (parent_id = user_id).
TCH/ADM: sees all school notifications.
Filters: ?filter[event_ref__like]=grade
Sort: ?sort=-created_at (default)
Search: ?search=bulletin

parameters.name: sort

schema.description: Sort fields, comma-separated. Prefix with - for desc. E.g. -created_at,title
schema.title: Sort

parameters.description: Sort fields, comma-separated. Prefix with - for desc. E.g. -created_at,title

parameters.name: search

schema.description: Full-text search term. Searches across title, description, body fields.
schema.title: Search

parameters.description: Full-text search term. Searches across title, description, body fields.

get.description:
List consent preferences.

PAR: sees own consents only.
ADM: sees all school consents.

put.description:
Update a consent preference.

PAR: can only update own consents.
ADM: can update any consent in the school.

get.description:
List parent feed items with filtering, sorting, and full-text search.

PAR only — sees their own feed items.
Filters: ?filter[source_type]=grade&filter[student_id]=...
Sort: ?sort=-created_at (default)
Search: ?search=bulletin
Legacy param student_id still supported.

List users in the authenticated user's school with optional search/filter.

schema.description: Search by name or email

Change a user's role. ADM only. Valid roles: TCH, PAR, STD.

parameters.name: status

schema.description: Filter by status
schema.title: Status

parameters.description: Filter by status

post.description:
Bulk-create user accounts from a list.

For each entry: creates user with a generated password, membership, and
invitation code (consumed immediately). Skips entries where email already exists.
Returns created accounts with their temporary passwords.
Admin manually links a parent to a student within the same school.

parameters.name: parent_user_id

schema.description: Parent user ID
schema.title: Parent User Id

parameters.name: child_user_id

schema.description: Student user ID
schema.title: Child User Id

schema.description: Filter by parent user ID

schema.title: Cursor

delete.description: Soft-revoke a parent-child link (sets status to 'revoked').

get.description: List classes assigned to the authenticated teacher with student and course counts.
List enrolled students in a class. Teacher must be assigned to the class.

List submissions for the teacher's courses/assignments.

Filters: assignment_id, course_id, status.
Only returns submissions for courses owned by the teacher.

parameters.name: status

schema.description: Filter: submitted, graded, draft
schema.title: Status

parameters.description: Filter: submitted, graded, draft

get.description: List active periods for the teacher's school (used in attendance form).

post.description:
Submit text for AI-assisted writing feedback.

Guardrails (G3):
  - Input sanitized for PII (POL-G3-001)
  - Opt-out checked (POL-G3-002)
  - Output validated (POL-G3-003)
  - Audit trail recorded

Update AI personalization opt-out preference.

Parents can opt out of AI personalization for their children.
Per G3.3: POST /ai/preferences/opt-out is contractual in OpenAPI.
Idempotent — upserts the preference.

get.description:
Get personalized learning recommendations.

Guardrails:
  - Opt-out checked (POL-G3-002): returns empty if opted out
  - Reason code mandatory on each recommendation (G3)

Get computed KPIs for the school (G1 catalog).

Returns KPI-G1-001 through KPI-G1-006 for the specified period.
ADM, DIR, TCH roles can access.

parameters.name: period

schema.description: Period in days
schema.title: Period

parameters.description: Period in days

get.description:
Get the analytics event schema registry.

Returns all known events with their schema versions and property whitelists.
Used for CI drift detection (S-146).

Export complete user data as JSON. Available to ADM or the user themselves.

Includes: profile, memberships, sessions, audit logs, submissions,
grades, notifications, invoices, payments, consent preferences.

post.description:
Anonymize user PII. ADM only. Keeps audit structure intact.

Replaces: email, full_name, phone with hashed values.
Revokes all sessions. Clears TOTP secrets and backup codes.
Does NOT delete audit log entries (required for compliance).

get.description:
Full consent change history for a user. ADM or self.

Returns all audit log entries where the target is a consent preference
belonging to this user, plus current consent preferences.
Return the authenticated user's profile including role-specific data.

200.description: User data with role-specific profile fields

put.description:
Update the authenticated user's role-specific profile fields.

Accepts role-specific fields depending on user role (STD, PAR, TCH).
Creates the profile row if it doesn't exist yet.

get.summary: Admin: get any user's role-specific profile
get.description:
Admin-only: read any user's combined profile (user + role-specific data).

Enforces school boundary — admin can only view users in their own school.

200.description: User data with role-specific profile fields

get.description:
Return the list of children linked to the authenticated parent.

Only accessible by users with PAR role. Returns basic child info + student profile.

post.description: CONTENT_MGR creates platform-wide content (school_id=NULL).

get.description: CONTENT_MGR lists all platform content (school_id IS NULL).
CONTENT_MGR reviews teacher submissions. Filter by status/subject/level/school.

post.description:
CONTENT_MGR approves or rejects a teacher submission.

Approve: creates platform copy, awards points, notifies teacher.
Reject: updates status, notifies teacher with feedback.

get.description: Teachers browse platform-wide (school_id=NULL) + their school's published content.

post.description: Teacher assigns a content item to one of their classes.

delete.description: Teacher removes a content assignment from their class.

post.description: Teacher submits their school-scoped content for platform promotion.

get.description: Teacher sees status of their submitted content.
Students (and parents/teachers) see content assigned to a class.

post.description: CONTENT_MGR creates platform-wide (school_id=NULL), TCH creates school-scoped.

get.description: List quizzes visible to the user (platform + school-scoped).
Get quiz detail. Students don't see correct_answer or explanation.

put.description: Update quiz metadata and questions. Only if status is DRAFT.

post.description: Publish a draft quiz. Must have at least one question.
Student starts a new quiz attempt. Checks max_attempts limit.
Student submits an answer for one question in an active attempt.
Student submits attempt. Triggers auto-grading for all responses.

get.description: View graded attempt results with explanations.
Teacher/CONTENT_MGR sees aggregate quiz performance stats.

post.description:
Create one or more timetable slots.

Accepts either a single slot or a bulk request with up to 50 slots.
Validates:
1. Class exists and belongs to the same school
2. Academic year exists and belongs to the same school
3. end_time > start_time
4. No overlapping slots for the same class or teacher

get.description:
List timetable slots with optional filters.

Filters: class_id, teacher_id, academic_year_id, day_of_week.
Always scoped to the user's school.

put.description:
Update an existing timetable slot.

Validates overlap after applying changes.

delete.description: Delete a timetable slot and all its exceptions (cascade).

get.description: Get the weekly timetable for a class, with exception overlays.

parameters.name: target_date

schema.description: Any date in the target week (defaults to today)
schema.title: Target Date

parameters.description: Any date in the target week (defaults to today)

get.description: Get the weekly timetable for a teacher, with exception overlays.

parameters.name: target_date

schema.description: Any date in the target week (defaults to today)
schema.title: Target Date

parameters.description: Any date in the target week (defaults to today)

get.description:
Get the weekly timetable for the current user.

- TCH: returns teacher's own timetable
- STD: returns student's class timetable
- PAR: returns first child's class timetable (use /class/{id}/weekly for specific child)
- ADM/DIR: returns empty (use explicit class/teacher views)

parameters.name: target_date

schema.description: Any date in the target week (defaults to today)
schema.title: Target Date

parameters.description: Any date in the target week (defaults to today)

post.description:
Create an exception for a timetable slot (cancel, substitute, room change).

Validates:
1. Slot exists and belongs to the same school
2. No duplicate exception for same slot+date
3. SUBSTITUTED requires substitute_teacher_id
4. ROOM_CHANGED requires new_room

get.description: List timetable exceptions with optional filters.

post.description:
Create a fee structure for the school.

Validates:
1. Academic year exists and belongs to the same school

get.description:
List fee structures with optional filters.

Always scoped to the user's school.

post.description:
Assign a fee structure to a student.

Validates:
1. Fee structure exists and belongs to the same school
2. Student exists
3. No duplicate assignment for the same fee + student

get.description:
List fee assignments with optional filters.

PAR can only see assignments for their own children.

post.description:
Bulk assign a fee structure to all students in a class or level.

Provide class_id OR level (not both). Skips students who already have
this fee assigned.

Generate invoices for all active fee assignments of a fee structure.

For each assigned student:
1. Find their parent (via parent_child_links)
2. Calculate amount after discount
3. Create invoice + line item

Skips students without a linked parent.
Due dates must satisfy due_date >= issued_date.

Start a new conversation between participants.

ABAC enforced: parents can only message teachers of their children's
classes, and vice versa. ADM/DIR can message anyone.

get.description:
List conversations the current user participates in.

Ordered by most recent message. Cursor-based pagination.
List messages in a conversation (newest first). Cursor-based pagination.

post.description:
Send a message in an existing conversation.

User must be a participant. Pushes WebSocket event to other participants.
Mark a message (and all prior messages) as read in a conversation.

get.summary: Get read receipts for a conversation
get.description: Get read receipts for messages in a conversation.

parameters.name: message_id

schema.description: Filter by specific message
schema.title: Message Id

parameters.description: Filter by specific message

post.description:
Create a new announcement as a draft.

Must be published separately via POST /announcements/{id}/publish.

get.description:
List announcements.

ADM/DIR: see all announcements (including drafts).
TCH/PAR/STD: see only PUBLISHED announcements targeting their role.

put.description: Update a draft announcement. Cannot update published announcements.

post.description:
Publish an announcement and send notifications to targeted users.

1. Changes status to PUBLISHED.
2. Creates a Notification for each targeted user.
3. Pushes WebSocket event to online users.

get.description:
Full student progress dashboard with grade trends, content completion,
activity scores, attendance rates, and assessment results.

ABAC enforced:
- STD: own progress only
- PAR: linked children only
- TCH: students in assigned classes only
- ADM/DIR: any student in school

All data is cached in Redis (15-min TTL).
Response format: chart-ready (labels + datasets arrays).

Class-wide progress summary for teachers and admins.

Shows per-student metrics and class-level averages.
ABAC: TCH (assigned classes), ADM/DIR (any class in school).

Response format: chart-ready (labels + datasets arrays).

Shortcut for students to view their own progress.

Equivalent to GET /progress/student/{my_user_id}.
Only available for STD role (other roles should use /student/{id}).

200.description: Chart-ready progress data for current student

get.description:
Parent view: progress summary for all linked children.

Shows key metrics per child with comparison charts.
Only available for PAR role.

200.description: Chart-ready progress overview for all linked children

get.summary: Get active features for current user
get.description:
Returns list of feature keys that are enabled for the current user's
school and role. Used by frontend for conditional feature rendering.
List all feature toggles. Only SYS and CONTENT_MGR roles.

post.description: Create a new feature toggle. Only SYS and CONTENT_MGR roles can manage toggles.

get.description: Get a single feature toggle by ID.

put.description: Update a feature toggle. Partial update — only provided fields are changed.

delete.description: Delete a feature toggle. Removes it entirely (not soft-delete).

opt_out.title: Opt Out
opt_out.description: True to opt out, False to opt back in

target_user_id.title: Target User Id
